perturbation.original_text: 1.1 "Spinrecords.com Brand Features" means the SpinRecords.com name and logo and other trademarks, trade names and service names including but not limited to those set forth on Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein --------- by reference and all Intellectual Property Rights related thereto. SpinRecords.com Brand Features shall include any derivatives, improvements or modifications thereto or thereof and any Intellectual Property rights related thereto.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.1 "Spinrecords.com Brand Features" means the SpinRecords.com name and logo as specifically listed and visually represented in Exhibit A attached hereto and incorporated herein --------- by reference and all Intellectual Property Rights directly related to those exact representations. SpinRecords.com Brand Features shall NOT include any derivatives, improvements or modifications thereto or thereof.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of "Spinrecords.com Brand Features" includes derivatives, improvements, or modifications. The modified definition explicitly excludes these, creating a contradiction within Section 1 concerning the scope of the licensed brand features, and potential disputes around usage rights if NetTaxi makes alterations to the logo or name.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 Licenses by SpinRecords.com to NETTAXI. SpinRecords.com hereby ------------------------------------------- grants NETTAXI a nonexclusive, worldwide, nontransferable, revocable, royalty free license to display and distribute the, and make derivative works from the SpinRecords.com Brand Features and any enhancements, modifications or improvements thereto as necessary to carry out the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text includes derivatives, improvements, or modifications of Spinrecords.com Brand Features, while the changed text explicitly excludes them. This directly contradicts section 3.2, which grants NetTaxi the license to make derivative works from the SpinRecords.com Brand Features and any enhancements, modifications or improvements thereto. This contradiction creates uncertainty regarding usage rights and is a strong in-text contradiction as it directly impacts the scope of the license granted.

perturbation.original_text: 2.1 The SpinRecords.com Pages. SpinRecords.com shall brand the --------------------------- Source: RAE SYSTEMS INC, 10-Q, 11/14/2000 SpinRecords.com Pages with the NETTAXI Brand Features in the manner set forth in the Statement of Work. NETTAXI will provide electronic copies of the NETTAXI Brand Features upon SpinRecords.com's request. All Spinrecords.com Pages shall display appropriate intellectual property legends, including but not limited to the copyright notice and trademark references.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.1 The SpinRecords.com Pages. SpinRecords.com may brand the --------------------------- Source: RAE SYSTEMS INC, 10-Q, 11/14/2000 SpinRecords.com Pages with the NETTAXI Brand Features in the manner set forth in the Statement of Work. NETTAXI will provide electronic copies of the NETTAXI Brand Features upon SpinRecords.com's request. All Spinrecords.com Pages shall display appropriate intellectual property legends, including but not limited to the copyright notice and trademark references.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall" to "may" in Section 2.1 introduces ambiguity regarding SpinRecords.com's obligation to brand its pages with NETTAXI Brand Features. While the Statement of Work might specify branding requirements, this modification allows SpinRecords.com to argue that branding is discretionary. This creates an in-text contradiction with the intent of the agreement as a co-branding partnership.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.4 Marketing by NETTAXI. Throughout the term of this Agreement, ----------------------- NETTAXI shall use reasonable commercial efforts to market the SpinRecords.com Brand Features in order to maximize the number of visitors to the SpinRecords.com Pages in accordance with the Statement of Work. The parties shall review the Statement of Work and Spinrecords.com's marketing activities on a quarterly basis in order to assess the performance and agree upon additional activities, if necessary, in order to increase usage of the pages.
perturbation.justification: Changing "shall" to "may" introduces ambiguity regarding SpinRecords.com's obligation to brand its pages with NETTAXI Brand Features as indicated in Section 2.1. This directly conflicts with Section 2.4, which states "NETTAXI shall use reasonable commercial efforts to market the SpinRecords.com Brand Features." If SpinRecords.com is not obligated to brand its pages with NETTAXI features, then NETTAXI's marketing efforts are undermined, creating a clear and impactful contradiction. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition, making enforcement difficult because the obligations are no longer consistent.

perturbation.original_text: 5.5 SpinRecords.com Rate. In full consideration for the rights granted --------------------- by NETTAXI, SpinRecords.com agrees to pay NETTAXI fifty (50%) percent of ad revenue at an average rate no lower then $6.50 per one thousand impressions (CPM) payable to NETTAXI which results when the SpinRecords.com Advertising Revenue is multiplied by SpinRecords.com's AR Share. SpinRecords.com shall be responsible for payment of all taxes based on the Advertising Revenue except taxes based on NETTAXI's income.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.5 SpinRecords.com Rate. In full consideration for the rights granted --------------------- by NETTAXI, SpinRecords.com agrees to pay NETTAXI fifty (50%) percent of ad revenue at an average rate no lower then $6.50 per one thousand impressions (CPM) payable to NETTAXI if SpinRecords.com deems such payment appropriate, which results when the SpinRecords.com Advertising Revenue is multiplied by SpinRecords.com's AR Share. SpinRecords.com shall be responsible for payment of all taxes based on the Advertising Revenue except taxes based on NETTAXI's income.
perturbation.explanation: By adding "if SpinRecords.com deems such payment appropriate," Section 5.5 now includes a discretionary clause, directly contradicting the initial statement that SpinRecords.com 'agrees to pay' NETTAXI a fixed percentage of revenue. This discretionary clause introduces uncertainty and allows SpinRecords.com to potentially avoid payment, undermining the financial agreement of the co-branding partnership. This can lead to disputes related to NETTAXI's expected revenue.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.7 Payment Schedule. The parties shall make such payments within ----------------- thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter for the Advertising Revenue invoiced during such calendar quarter.
perturbation.justification: The original text in Section 5.5, 'SpinRecords.com agrees to pay NETTAXI fifty (50%) percent of ad revenue,' establishes a clear obligation for payment. The changed text introduces a discretionary element ('if SpinRecords.com deems such payment appropriate'), which directly contradicts the mandatory payment obligation. This contradiction undermines the certainty of payment outlined in Section 5.7, 'The parties shall make such payments within thirty (30) days of the end of each calendar quarter,' as SpinRecords.com can now potentially avoid payment at its discretion, rendering the payment schedule unenforceable.
